scenarios.title: Agent App Frameworks
scenarios.when_to_read: Building Python applications that connect LLMs to MCP servers, tools, workflows, and configuration using mcp-agent.
scenarios.role: Provides repo-specific guidance for mcp-agent's SDK, workflow patterns, MCP server integration, Temporal execution, CLI/cloud operations, and observability.
scenarios.read_when: The task mentions mcp-agent, MCPApp, Agent, AgentSpec, AugmentedLLM, RequestParams, mcp_agent.config.yaml, mcp_agent.secrets.yaml, LLMRouter, ParallelLLM, Orchestrator, EvaluatorOptimizerLLM, FastMCP app servers, mcp-agent CLI, mcp-cloud, Temporal executor, or MCP Agent Cloud.
scenarios.best_for: Creating/debugging mcp-agent apps, composing workflow patterns, exposing apps as MCP servers, validating config, operating the CLI/cloud deployment flow, adding Temporal durability, and configuring logging/tracing/provider integrations.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only about generic MCP protocol usage without mcp-agent, or about another unrelated agent framework with no mcp-agent APIs/CLI/config.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `mcp-agent` when mcp-agent-specific package APIs, CLIs, config files, examples, or errors matter. Start at the root router, then follow the sub-skill matching the user-visible workflow rather than the source module name.

scenarios.title: MCP Server Apps
scenarios.when_to_read: Connecting to upstream MCP servers or exposing an mcp-agent application as an MCP server with tools, workflows, OAuth, sampling, or elicitation.
scenarios.role: Covers mcp-agent's MCP transport configuration, server registry, app server wrappers, OAuth/token storage, primitives, and safe client-install preparation.
scenarios.read_when: The task mentions MCP server config, stdio, SSE, streamable HTTP, websocket, roots, allowed_tools, create_mcp_server_for_app, workflows-run, workflows-get_status, OAuth MCP servers, token stores, sampling, elicitation, prompts, resources, or app-as-server behavior.
scenarios.best_for: Diagnosing mcp.servers entries, wiring Agent server_names, validating app server tool surfaces, handling OAuth/resource metadata, and preparing safe client install/deploy steps.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic CLI deployment with no MCP transport/auth detail; use the CLI/cloud route inside the same skill instead.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use the MCP server sub-skill for transport/auth/tool-surface reasoning, then switch to CLI/cloud operations only for command execution or hosted lifecycle tasks.

scenarios.title: Durable Agent Operations
scenarios.when_to_read: Running mcp-agent workflows as long-lived or resumable Temporal-backed services and operating them locally or through Cloud commands.
scenarios.role: Explains how mcp-agent maps decorators, workflow tasks, signals, retry policies, and workers onto Temporal while preserving local asyncio development patterns.
scenarios.read_when: The task mentions execution_engine temporal, TemporalExecutor, create_temporal_worker_for_app, workflow_task_modules, workflow_task_retry_policies, wait_for_signal, signal_workflow, pause/resume, long-running tools, or workflow resume/cancel.
scenarios.best_for: Converting workflow code to Temporal, writing worker skeletons, designing pause/resume approvals, setting retry policies, and deciding what needs a running Temporal service.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only needs in-process pattern choice or one-shot local generation; use workflow-patterns or core-sdk instead.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Start with durable-execution when durability/signals/workers are primary; route back to workflow-patterns for choosing the workflow shape and to CLI/cloud operations for hosted workflow commands.
